description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, featuring a polished, dark wood grain finish. The front panel is divided into two main sections: the upper section has a metallic or fabric speaker grille, and the lower section is black with two large cream-colored knobs on either side of a central frequency display. The frequency display is detailed with yellow and white markings, and there is a small rectangular button or switch above the right knob. The sides and back are plain, with the sides showing a black rectangular panel, likely for ventilation or speaker output, and the back is a solid black panel. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining both functional and decorative elements. This radio would typically be found in homes, cafes, or retro-themed urban environments.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a polished finish, featuring a front speaker grille, two large knobs, and a central frequency display. The sides and back are plain with black panels.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, two large knobs, a frequency display, and a small button or switch.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the wood grain body and a black rectangular panel, with no controls or display visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a solid black panel covering the entire back, with no visible controls or openings.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wood grain and a black rectangular panel.
